title: Au-Haidhausen

infobox.value: Inhabitants. | of which Foreigners | Area (ha.) | Inhabitants per ha. | Source

(German pronunciation: [[ˈaʊ haɪtˈhaʊzn̩]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German"); [Central Bavarian](/wiki/Central_Bavarian "Central Bavarian"): *Au-Haidhausn*) is the 5th borough of the [German](/wiki/Germany "Germany") city of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), [Bavaria](/wiki/Bavaria "Bavaria"). It is formed by the **[Au](/wiki/Au_(Munich) "Au (Munich)")** and **[Haidhausen](/wiki/Haidhausen_(Munich) "Haidhausen (Munich)")** districts.

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [48°07′55″N 11°35′20″E﻿ / ﻿48.13194°N 11.58889°E﻿ / 48.13194; 11.58889](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Au-Haidhausen&params=48_07_55_N_11_35_20_E_region:DE-BY_type:city_source:dewiki)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Borough of Munich, Germany

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/17/M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_05_%28Karte%29_-_Au_-_Haidhausen.png/250px-M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_05_%28Karte%29_-_Au_-_Haidhausen.png)](/wiki/File:M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_05_(Karte)_-_Au_-_Haidhausen.png)

Borough 5 **Au-Haidhausen**, Location in Munich

**Au-Haidhausen** (German pronunciation: [[ˈaʊ haɪtˈhaʊzn̩]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German"); [Central Bavarian](/wiki/Central_Bavarian "Central Bavarian"): *Au-Haidhausn*) is the 5th borough of the [German](/wiki/Germany "Germany") city of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), [Bavaria](/wiki/Bavaria "Bavaria"). It is formed by the **[Au](/wiki/Au_(Munich) "Au (Munich)")** and **[Haidhausen](/wiki/Haidhausen_(Munich) "Haidhausen (Munich)")** districts.

## Location

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Au-Haidhausen&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Location")]

Au lies opposite the Altstadt of the city on the easterly plain tract of the [Isar](/wiki/Isar "Isar"). Haidhausen is above Au on the Isar's uplands. Au-Haidhausen borders [Bogenhausen](/wiki/Bogenhausen "Bogenhausen") to the north, [Berg am Laim](/wiki/Berg_am_Laim "Berg am Laim") to the east, [Obergiesing](/wiki/Obergiesing "Obergiesing") to the south, level with the flow of the Isar at [Untergiesing](/wiki/Untergiesing-Harlaching "Untergiesing-Harlaching"), ending in the west at the river.

## History and description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Au-Haidhausen&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History and description")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/03/Gasteig_Philharmonie_14.jpg/250px-Gasteig_Philharmonie_14.jpg)](/wiki/File:Gasteig_Philharmonie_14.jpg)

[Gasteig](/wiki/Gasteig "Gasteig") cultural center

Au and Haidhausen used to be hostel areas for trade workers and day labourers outside of the medieval town walls of Munich. Both formerly independent municipalities were incorporated into the City of Munich on 1 October 1854 and developed into suburbs during the [Gründerzeit](/wiki/Gr%C3%BCnderzeit "Gründerzeit"). Many breweries were located on the high banks of the Isar, thus right on the edge of the terrace. These were good locations for deep natural water sources and beer cellars; for this reason, the name "Keller" (cellar) is still used to refer to the brewery-restaurants nearby. Near Rosenheimer Platz on Rosenheimer street stood the Bürgerbräukeller, the location of the first assassination attempt on [Adolf Hitler](/wiki/Adolf_Hitler "Adolf Hitler"). Today, one of the largest breweries in Munich can be found there: [Paulaner](/wiki/Paulaner "Paulaner"), in Au. Nearby, the [Gasteig](/wiki/Gasteig "Gasteig") marks the transition to the inner city, a cultural center which hosts the [Munich Philharmonic Orchestra](/wiki/Munich_Philharmonic "Munich Philharmonic"), parts of the [University of Music and Performing Arts Munich](/wiki/Hochschule_f%C3%BCr_Musik_und_Theater_M%C3%BCnchen "Hochschule für Musik und Theater München"), the [Volkshochschule](/wiki/Folk_high_school "Folk high school"), Munich's municipal library and important events such as the [Filmfest München](/wiki/Filmfest_M%C3%BCnchen "Filmfest München") and the [Munich Biennale](/wiki/Munich_Biennale "Munich Biennale").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a3/Bordeauxplatz_Haidhausen_M%C3%BCnchen_3.jpg/250px-Bordeauxplatz_Haidhausen_M%C3%BCnchen_3.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bordeauxplatz_Haidhausen_M%C3%BCnchen_3.jpg)

Neighborhood in Haidhausen

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fa/Luftbild_Haidhausen_in_M%C3%BCnchen.jpg/250px-Luftbild_Haidhausen_in_M%C3%BCnchen.jpg)](/wiki/File:Luftbild_Haidhausen_in_M%C3%BCnchen.jpg)

Au-Haidhausen enclosed by [Munich East station](/wiki/Munich_East_station "Munich East station") (top) and the [Isar](/wiki/Isar "Isar") (bottom)

Even though the structural integrity of buildings in Au was heavily compromised during the [Second World War](/wiki/Second_World_War "Second World War") and only a fifth of buildings dated before 1919 still stand, the historical townscape of Haidhausen remains largely intact. Of high constructional significance is the Franzosenviertel ("the French quarter"), so called because of given street names to locations of victory in battles of the [German-French War](/wiki/Franco-Prussian_War "Franco-Prussian War"). The existence of good condition housing lead to Haidhausen becoming the second largest area of redevelopment in the 1970s, alongside [Schwanthalerhöhe](/wiki/Schwanthalerh%C3%B6he "Schwanthalerhöhe"). This property redevelopment and revaluation triggered by the upgrade of accommodation lead to not only restructuring of a suburb to the outskirts of the area, but also a change of image, thus putting Haidhausen in competition with Schwabing as a trendy area to live in Munich.

The balanced mixture of accommodation and living in Au and Haidhausen has shifted towards housing; this is due to the outsourcing of otherwise bothersome trades. Most jobs in the district lie in service industries and other public areas.

Haidhausen accommodates the [Bayerische Landtag](/wiki/Landtag_of_Bavaria "Landtag of Bavaria") in the [Maximilianeum](/wiki/Maximilianeum "Maximilianeum"), the [Klinikum rechts der Isar](/wiki/Klinikum_rechts_der_Isar "Klinikum rechts der Isar") and the [Wiener Markt](/wiki/Wiener_Markt "Wiener Markt"). On the northern border the [Friedensengel](/w/index.php?title=Friedensengel&action=edit&redlink=1 "Friedensengel (page does not exist)") ("peace angel") towers above the city on the Isar plateau. If one follows [Prinzregentenstraße](/wiki/Prinzregentenstrasse_(Munich) "Prinzregentenstrasse (Munich)") from there to the east, around 100 metres along on the right hand side is the [Villa Stuck](/wiki/Villa_Stuck "Villa Stuck"), that the "Malerfürst" ("Painter prince") [Franz von Stuck](/wiki/Franz_von_Stuck "Franz von Stuck") allowed to be constructed according to his own blueprints. As of 2004 the entirely reconditioned buildings of the Villa and the former studio is now an art museum. Further east down Prinzregentenstraße lies the [Prinzregententheater](/wiki/Prinzregententheater "Prinzregententheater"), one of the most striking [Art Nouveau](/wiki/Art_Nouveau "Art Nouveau") theatre buildings in Germany; adjacent to which is the Prinzregentenstadion, which serves as a popular ice skating venue in winter. With the [Maria-Theresia-Gymnasium](/wiki/Maria-Theresia-Gymnasium "Maria-Theresia-Gymnasium") in the south the district also accommodates one of the few German Schools of Excellence.

With the Auer Dult taking place three times per year at the Mariahilfplatz, and the tapping of Bock at the Nockherberg in the Salvatorkeller of the Paulaner brewery on the *Hochstraße* (High street), rebuilt after heavy fire damage, Au has a significant amount of traditional daily culture.
